metadata.name: diracx-core
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)
metadata.summary: Common code used by all DiracX packages

functions.name: diracx.core.utils.dotenv_files_from_environment
documentation: Get the sorted list of .env files to use for configuration.

functions.name: diracx.core.utils.serialize_credentials
documentation:
Serialize DiracX client credentials to a string.

This method is separated from write_credentials to allow for DIRAC to be
able to serialize credentials for inclusion in the proxy file.

functions.name: diracx.core.utils.read_credentials
documentation: Read credentials from a file.

functions.name: diracx.core.utils.write_credentials
documentation: Write credentials received in dirax_preferences.credentials_path.

classes.name: diracx.core.exceptions.PendingAuthorizationError
documentation: Used to signal the device flow the authentication is still ongoing.

classes.name: diracx.core.exceptions.ExpiredFlowError
documentation: Used only for the Device Flow when the polling is expired.

classes.name: diracx.core.exceptions.ConfigurationError
documentation: Used whenever we encounter a problem with the configuration.

classes.name: diracx.core.exceptions.BadConfigurationVersion
documentation: The requested version is not known.

classes.name: diracx.core.exceptions.InvalidQueryError
documentation: It was not possible to build a valid database query from the given input.

modules.name: diracx.core.properties
documentation:
Just listing the possible Properties
This module contains list of Properties that can be assigned to users and groups.

functions.name: diracx.core.preferences.get_diracx_preferences
documentation: Caches the preferences.

methods.name: diracx.core.config.schema.BaseModel.legacy_adaptor
documentation: Apply transformations to interpret the legacy DIRAC CFG format.

methods.name: diracx.core.config.schema.RegistryConfig.sub_from_preferred_username
documentation:
Get the user sub from the preferred username.

TODO: This could easily be cached or optimised

modules.name: diracx.core.s3
documentation: Utilities for interacting with S3-compatible storage.

functions.name: diracx.core.s3.s3_bucket_exists
documentation: Check if a bucket exists in S3.

functions.name: diracx.core.s3.s3_object_exists
documentation: Check if an object exists in an S3 bucket.

functions.name: diracx.core.s3.generate_presigned_upload
documentation:
Generate a presigned URL and fields for uploading a file to S3.

The signature is restricted to only accept data with the given checksum and size.

functions.name: diracx.core.s3.b16_to_b64
documentation: Convert hexadecimal encoded data to base64 encoded data.

modules.name: diracx.core.config
documentation:
This module implements the logic of the configuration server side.

This is where all the backend abstraction and the caching logic takes place.

functions.name: diracx.core.config._apply_default_scheme
documentation: Applies the default git+file:// scheme if not present.

classes.name: diracx.core.config.ConfigSource

methods.name: diracx.core.config.ConfigSource.latest_revision
documentation:
Must return:
* a unique hash as a string, representing the last version
* a datetime object corresponding to when the version dates.

methods.name: diracx.core.config.ConfigSource.read_raw
documentation:
Return the Config object that corresponds to the
specific hash
The `modified` parameter is just added as a attribute to the config.

methods.name: diracx.core.config.ConfigSource.__init_subclass__
documentation: Keep a record of <scheme: class>.

methods.name: diracx.core.config.ConfigSource.create_from_url
documentation:
Factory method to produce a concrete instance depending on
the backend URL scheme.

methods.name: diracx.core.config.ConfigSource.read_config
documentation:
:raises:
git.exc.BadName if version does not exist

This class is the abstract base class that should be used everywhere
throughout the code.
It acts as a factory for concrete implementations
See the abstractmethods to implement a concrete class.

classes.name: diracx.core.config.BaseGitConfigSource

methods.name: diracx.core.config.BaseGitConfigSource.read_raw
documentation: :param: hexsha commit hash

Base class for the git based config source
The caching is based on 2 caches:
* TTL to find the latest commit hashes
* LRU to keep in memory the last few versions.

classes.name: diracx.core.config.LocalGitConfigSource
documentation:
The configuration is stored on a local git repository
When running on multiple servers, the filesystem must be shared.

classes.name: diracx.core.config.RemoteGitConfigSource

methods.name: diracx.core.config.RemoteGitConfigSource._pull
documentation: Git pull from remote repo.
Use a remote directory as a config source.

functions.name: diracx.core.settings._maybe_load_key_from_file
documentation: Load private keys from files if needed.

functions.name: diracx.core.settings._apply_default_scheme
documentation: Applies the default file:// scheme if not present.

methods.name: diracx.core.settings.ServiceSettingsBase.lifetime_function
documentation: A context manager that can be used to run code at startup and shutdown.

classes.name: diracx.core.settings.DevelopmentSettings
documentation: Settings for the Development Configuration that can influence run time.

classes.name: diracx.core.models.SortSpec
documentation: TODO: TypedDict vs pydantic?

inner_classes.name: diracx.core.models.SetJobStatusReturn.SetJobStatusReturnSuccess
documentation: Successful new status change.

class_variables.name: diracx.core.models.UserInfo.sub
documentation: dirac generated vo:sub

functions.name: diracx.core.extensions.extensions_by_priority
documentation: Yield extension module names in order of priority.

functions.name: diracx.core.extensions.select_from_extension
documentation:
Select entry points by group and name, in order of priority.

Similar to `importlib.metadata.entry_points.select` except only modules
found in `DIRACX_EXTENSIONS` are considered and return order is sorted
from highest to lowest priority.
